calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-19-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Judith Baer for and with Bernadette Burnett (Petitioner/Administrator).
Anthony Danielson for Lakeview Trust #124 (Objector).

Mr. Danielson stated that he filed a reply to the response. Ms. Baer stated that she just saw the reply this morning. The Court informed the parties that it was inclined to grant the petition and confirm that the assignments are executed and on file, but it will now review and consider the reply. The First Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-17-2017 Continued To May-08-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Judith Baer for and with Bernadette Burnett (Administrator).
Anthony Danielson appeared by telephone for Lakeview Trust #124 (Objector/Party in Interest).

Mr. Danielson stated that he filed an objection on Friday afternoon and that he provided it by e-mail to Counsel. Ms. Baer requested three weeks to review and reply to the objection. The First and Final Account and Report of Administrator is continued to May 8, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-24-2017 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-13-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Judith Baer, for and with petitioner Bernadette Burnett; Erin Scharg (by CourtCall) for petitioner Saim Chaudrey.

The Court observed that with regard to the petition of Saim Chaudrey issues remained to be addressed, and asked Ms. Scharg to address why the petition of Ms. Burnett should not be granted. Ms. Scharg acknowledged that the petition of Ms. Burnett had priority and there was no reason it should not be granted. The Court ststed that he would need a supplement to Ms. Burnett's petition with the information which was omitted from paragraph 8 of the petition to appoint Ms. Burnett as administrator. He added that if the supplement was received by the court by September 20, 2016 and gives a satisfactory statement of the omitted information, the petition of Ms. Burnett would be granted. The Court granted the petition to appoint Bernadette Burnett as administrator of the estate of Barbara Alexander subject to receipt and review of the supplement to the petition. The petition by Saim Chaudrey is denied.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-03-2016 Continued To Aug-31-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Eason (via court call) for (petitioner) Sam Chaudhry. Bernadette Burnett (administrator for Grace McMullen estate) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that this is a petition for letters of administration with the current administrator seeking to nominate a successor, that there are procedural issues, that the administrator has assigned her interest and does not have priority to make a nomination, that the nominee has not provided a residence address, that it also pointed to an assignment signed by Anjanette Black who has been deceased for 10 years, that there are problems with the petition as presented and asked Ms. Burnett if she is here to object. Ms. Burnett stated that she does object, that she does not see how the administrator can sign over her shares, that the continuances are costing a lot of money and clarified that Anjanette died last year not 10 years ago. The Court stated that the petition stands where it is at the moment, that if Mr. Eason wants to pursue he may need to notice the Public Administrator, that Ms. Burnett's objections will need to be in writing, that Ms. Burnett could be brought in as an administrator and that there are a number of different ways to unravel the knot. Mr. Eason stated that he would like a continuance to provide notice to the Public Adminstrator and that obtaining the residence address of Mr. Chaudhry is an issue. The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to August 31, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court instructed Mr. Eason to clear up the issues raised in the examiner's notes and for Ms. Burnett to file her objections no later that August 24, 2016.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-11-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Judith Baer, for and with petitioner Bernadette Burnett; Verleana Green for
Jewel Black Osborne

Ms. Green stated that Sherrell Gillispie would be filing a dismissal of her petition, which would then leave two before the Court. She stated that her client, petitioner Jewel Black Osborne, is the daughter of the decedent, and therefor has statutory priority for appointment as administrator over the decedent's sister Bernadette Burnett. She stated that Jewel had not previously filed a petition for appointment as she had been unaware of her priority. Ms. Baer responded that while Ms. Osborne had statutory priority, she would not be appropriate to serve as administrator as she had proved herself untruthful, and opined that she would not faithfully carry out the duties as administrator of the decedent's estate. Ms. Baer stated that she had been attempting for over five years to get Ms. Osborne to petition for appointment as administrator, but that she had refused take any action until Ms. Burnett had finally done so. She also stated that she had been informed by Ms. Osborne that she was not residing at the address she provided to Ms. Baer as her home address. Ms. Green stated that petitioner Burnett is residing in the home which is the sole asset of the estate, and that she would have a conflict of interest with the other heirs. She also stated that Ms. Osborne's address was listed in her petition. Ms. Baer replied that Ms. Osborne herself had informed Ms. Baer that the address given by Ms. Osborne is not where she resides.
The matter was taken under submission.
Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported).

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-30-2015 Continued To Nov-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Judith Baer for (petitioner) Bernadette Burnett who is present in court. Jewell Black (decedent's daughter) in Propria Persona.

Ms. Black stated that she is here for Ms. Gillispie who could not be present and that she also objects. The Court stated that Ms. Black could not appear for Ms. Gillispie, asked if it was her intention to file additional objections and if she understood that this proceeding has nothing to do with the property in Texas. Ms. Black stated that she did not want the estate opened here. The Court stated that it has to be here as there is California property. Ms. Baer stated that Ms. Gillispie has already filed her petition. Ms. Black stated that her sister is unable to travel. Ms. Baer stated that they are under a court order to close the estate and to get this one opened, that she was not at the last hearing as the matter had been pre-granted and that Ms. Black was present at the last hearing. The Court stated that it wanted to make it clear to Ms. Black and her sister that the court needs probate opened in California, that there is no way to avoid it, that the court is unlikely to appoint an unrepresented administrator, that it will give one opportunity for her to file, reiterated that this has nothing to do with the Texas property and that there is no way to avoid probate here, that anything she wishes to file must be in writing and filed no later than October 27, 2015 and urged her to give careful consideration to what the court has said. The two petitions for letters of administration were continued to November 3, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputies Watts & Siu. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-31-2015 Continued To Sep-30-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jewel Black & Sherrell Gillispie (daughters/objectors) in Propria Persona.

This matter had been pre-granted. Ms. Black that their older sister had past away on August 16, 2015. Ms. Gillispie stated that they filed a petition this morning, that their Aunt opened the case without their consent, that the attorney had them sign away their rights, that they received notice late and that they reside in Texas. Ms. Black clarified that not all of the siblings signed the consents, that their mother died in Texas, that there are children from a prior marriage and that they did not want the estate probated at this time because their elderly stepfather is still living in the house and they want him to be able to stay there for the remainder of his life. The Court stated that they will give them additional time and suggested that they obtain counsel. The petition for letters of administration was continued to September 30, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to be heard with the petition filed this morning.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
